title: Portfolio Platform
description: A modern fullstack portfolio built with Next.js, .NET and Docker to present software engineering work, technical positioning and future case studies.
summary: A personal portfolio built as a modern fullstack experience to present software engineering work, technical positioning and future case studies.
overview: This project is the foundation of my public engineering portfolio. It is designed to present my profile, skills, technical experiments and documented projects in a clear, scalable and recruiter-friendly way.
purpose: The goal is to create a polished web experience that works both as a professional presentation and as a technical playground for modern frontend architecture, .NET API design, Dockerized development, routing, design systems and AI-assisted workflows.
technicalHighlights: SPA-style navigation and dynamic project routing
implementationNotes: Projects are rendered dynamically from backend-provided data, allowing new projects to be added without redesigning the page. Each project card routes to a dynamic detail page using a slug-based route such as /projects/portfolio-platform.
nextSteps: Connect GitHub and live demo links when available

title: Data Migration & SQL Modeling
description: Case study about transforming document-based monthly data into a relational daily model, focusing on indexing, query performance and scalable reporting.
summary: A data-focused case study about reshaping monthly document-style records into a relational model prepared for daily reporting and maintainable queries.
overview: This case study documents a migration-oriented data modeling exercise focused on replacing brittle document-shaped records with clearer relational structures.
purpose: The purpose is to show practical judgment around schema design, indexing, migration scripts and reporting-oriented query patterns without depending on a public production dataset.
implementationNotes: The project should be documented as a case study first, then expanded with anonymized schema examples and migration notes when the content is ready.
nextSteps: Prepare repository material if it can be shared safely

title: API Performance Optimization
description: Technical write-up about improving backend response times through query optimization, parallelism, caching strategy and cleaner service boundaries.
summary: A backend performance case study focused on API boundaries, query behavior, caching decisions and maintainable service design.
overview: This project explores how backend APIs can become easier to reason about when query work, caching decisions and service responsibilities are made explicit.
purpose: The purpose is to document a realistic performance-improvement workflow without publishing fake benchmarks or unsupported production claims.
implementationNotes: The write-up should focus on decision-making, tradeoffs and maintainability. Any future metrics should only be added if they come from a real measured scenario.
nextSteps: Include real measurements only when available

title: Business Dashboard Interface
description: Frontend case study focused on dashboards, filters, charts, data visualization and responsive UI for business decision-making.
summary: A frontend-oriented dashboard case study about organizing dense business data into a responsive and readable interface.
overview: This case study focuses on building dashboard interfaces that make filtering, scanning and comparison easier for recurring business workflows.
purpose: The purpose is to document frontend architecture and UI decisions for data-heavy screens without adding fake business outcomes.
implementationNotes: Future examples should use safe sample data and focus on interface structure, accessibility and state management.

title: AI-Assisted Development Workflow
description: Experiment documenting how AI tools can support analysis, prototyping, refactoring and code review while preserving engineering judgment.
summary: An experiment about using AI tools as part of a disciplined software engineering workflow instead of as a replacement for review and design judgment.
overview: This project documents practical AI-assisted development patterns across analysis, prototyping, refactoring and review.
purpose: The purpose is to show how AI tools can improve throughput while keeping architecture decisions, validation and accountability with the engineer.
implementationNotes: This project should remain grounded in real workflow examples and avoid broad claims about productivity unless they can be supported.

title: Architecture Refactoring Notes
description: Technical notes about improving maintainability by separating responsibilities, reducing coupling and organizing code around clearer boundaries.
summary: Architecture notes focused on maintainability, clearer module boundaries and incremental refactoring of application responsibilities.
overview: This project collects architectural notes about moving from loosely organized code toward clearer boundaries and more explicit responsibilities.
purpose: The purpose is to document practical refactoring judgment: what to move, what to leave alone and how to improve maintainability incrementally.
implementationNotes: The project should be expanded with concrete examples only when they can be shown without exposing private code or inventing context.
nextSteps: Connect examples to project detail pages
